mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding corporate output and revenues, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth months do not yield significantly higher 20d returns than flat/negative months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.3 percentage point or greater rise in the unemployment rate over three months signals an inflection in the labor market that precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of walk-forward cases, or Rank IC turns positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a rising federal funds rate and simultaneously declining industrial production creates a monetary-policy-overkill condition where discount rates rise while cash-flow growth stalls, jointly driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal underperforms the single-series FEDFUNDS model in walk-forward RMSE, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, acceleration in consumer price inflation above 2.5% annualized over three months forces expectations of more aggressive Fed hikes, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified tightening cycles, CPIAUCSL acceleration fails to predict negative 20d returns (mean return positive or t-stat > -1.0) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month strength in retail sales captures contemporaneous consumer demand that immediately flows through to revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS months fail to outperform negative RSAFS months over 5-day returns (Mann-Whitney p > 0.05), or Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below its 12-month trailing average signals weakening construction activity and household formation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST below-average readings coincide with positive 20d returns in more than 55 percent of walk-forward triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates durable income and consumption trajectories that underpin corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS growth fails to positively predict 60d returns (rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales confirm a coordinated expansion in both supply and final demand, generating strongly positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint up-move fails to produce higher average 5d returns than either series alone (paired t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.
